title: Array Methods
content:
### Array Methods

JavaScript arrays have **powerful built-in methods** for manipulation.

---

### Add/Remove Elements

| Method | Description | Example | Result |
|--------|-------------|---------|--------|
| `push(item)` | Add to **end** | `arr.push(4)` | Returns new length |
| `pop()` | Remove from **end** | `arr.pop()` | Returns removed item |
| `unshift(item)` | Add to **start** | `arr.unshift(0)` | Returns new length |
| `shift()` | Remove from **start** | `arr.shift()` | Returns removed item |
| `splice(i, n)` | Remove/replace at index | `arr.splice(1, 1)` | Returns removed items |

---

### Transform Arrays

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `concat(arr)` | Join two arrays | `[1,2].concat([3,4])` |
| `slice(i, j)` | Extract a portion | `[1,2,3].slice(0, 2)` |
| `join(sep)` | Join elements into string | `["a","b"].join("-")` |
| `reverse()` | Reverse the order | `[1,2,3].reverse()` |
| `sort()` | Sort alphabetically | `[3,1,2].sort()` |
| `indexOf(item)` | Find position | `[1,2,3].indexOf(2)` |
| `includes(item)` | Check if contains | `[1,2,3].includes(2)` |

---

### Spread Operator (`...`)

A modern way to combine arrays:

```js
let arr1 = [1, 2];
let arr2 = [3, 4];
let combined = [...arr1, ...arr2]; // [1, 2, 3, 4]
```

> **Mutating vs Non-mutating:** `push`, `pop`, `sort`, `reverse`, `splice` **change** the original array. `concat`, `slice`, `join`, `includes`, `indexOf` do **not**.

quiz.question: Which method adds an item to the end of an array?
